steps.title: Recognize what relationship the transition must express
steps.content: The passage first explains what Roman numerals indicate, then immediately states a consequence: an incorrect assumption you shouldn't make. The transition must bridge these two ideas as a logical result.

steps.title: Match the relationship to the correct transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is consequence — the sentence establishes that Roman numerals indicate naming order, not proximity, so the natural logical outcome is: you should not assume Helike is the 45th-closest moon.
  • Choice A — "however,": signals contrast or contradiction between two ideas, but the incorrect assumption follows logically from the numbering — it is a predictable misconception, not an arbitrary counterpoint. ✗
  • Choice B — "finally,": signals the last item in a series, but no series has been introduced — there is only one explanatory sentence before the blank. ✗
  • Choice C — "then,": signals a logical consequence, meaning "given what was just stated, it follows that..." — the incorrect assumption flows directly from the numbering system. ✓
  • Choice D — "moreover,": signals an additional, reinforcing point, but the sentence after the blank does not add new information — it restates what the reader should not infer. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
